summary: Make connect throw consistently across environments
description:
h3. Use Case
As a... Node.js driver user
I want... MongoClient.connect() to throw regardless of configuration if the host does not exist
So that... I can rely on the error to inform my application of the incorrect configuration

h3. User Experience
 * _What is the desired/expected outcome for the user once this ticket is implemented?_
** The MongoClient.connect function reliably identifies if a host is unable to be connected to

h3. Dependencies
 * Compass & Data Explorer connection latency improvements

h3. Risks/Unknowns
 * _What could go wrong while implementing this change? (e.g., performance, inadvertent behavioral changes in adjacent functionality, existing tech debt, etc)_
** It is a change in behavior, auth-less use is going to run a checkOut when it didn't previously, but now that we've removed "ping" already we will only be running a handshake and populating the pool by 1 so the next operation can use the work done in that step. 
** Additionally, the __skipPingOnConnect flag is still used to bypass the checkOut when we're calling connect for a command (auto-connect) so we don't perform a double checkout.
 * _Is there an opportunity for better cross-driver alignment or testing in this area?_
** connect is specific to Node
 * _Is there an opportunity to improve existing documentation on this subject?_
** The connect method should clarify what it represents to succeed or fail in ts-doc

h3. Acceptance Criteria
h4. Implementation Requirements
* Remove the requirement for credentials to run the checkOut "test" for connectivity


h4. Testing Requirements
* Verify LB=true and noauth throws if the host does not exist
** _Note:_ we don't need the topology for this since it is a failure case


h4. Documentation Requirements
 * API Docs on connect

h4. Follow Up Requirements
 * None
* _if node behavior differs/will differ from other drivers, confirm with dbx devs what standard to aim for and what plan, if any, exists to reconcile the diverging behavior moving forward_

summary: dropCollection should return false and not throw on ns not found errors
description:
h3. Use Case

As a node driver engineer,
I want the dropCollection method to suppress ns not found errors,
So that the unified spec test runner can work without suppressing these errors manually.
h3. User Impact

Users calling `dropCollection` will be impacted but the impact should be minimal.  Rather than throwing namespace not found errors, we will return `false`.  The current API for the dropCollection method returns a Promise<boolean>.  Consumers of this method may be checking for a truthy value indicating a successful drop if they care about it, but they could also have a catch block that handles this specific error, in which case this change would be breaking (as dropping a non-existent collection would now be indicated via the successful promise resolve with "false" instead of an error).

h3. Unknowns
 * Can this go out in a minor release? It's unlikely to really break anyone, but why take the chance given the change doesn't actually benefit our existing users.

h3. Acceptance Criteria
 * Remove the try-catch handling namespace not found errors from the unified runner.
 * Update dropCollection to return false for namespace not found errors.

h3. Documentation Requirements
 * _n/a_

summary: Wrap socket write in a try/catch to ensure errors can be properly wrapped
description:
As described in [https://www.mongodb.com/community/forums/t/mongodb-node-js-driver-vs-aws-lambda-with-provisioned-concurrency/326386], a Node.js [{{writeAfterFIN}} error|https://github.com/nodejs/node/blob/04c5a18e4cf157a3ac3435684f8ef4ee48a5364d/lib/net.js#L575-L585] can bubble up to the driver.

In NODE-6864, all socket errors were handled to be wrapped in MongoNetworkErrors, however this doesn't appear to be handled. 
h3. Use Case

As a... Node.js engineer
I want... any socket error to be converted to a MongoNetworkError
So that... driver retry and resume logic responds as expected
h3. User Experience
 * _What is the desired/expected outcome for the user once this ticket is implemented?_
 ** Socket error events are always converted to our MongoNetworkError class, because the driver will not retry plain runtime errors

h3. Dependencies
 * None

h3. Risks/Unknowns
 * _What could go wrong while implementing this change? (e.g., performance, inadvertent behavioral changes in adjacent functionality, existing tech debt, etc)_
 * _Is there an opportunity for better cross-driver alignment or testing in this area?_
 * _Is there an opportunity to improve existing documentation on this subject?_

h3. Acceptance Criteria
h4. Implementation Requirements
 * Identify the node socket APIs that are still not being wrapped (socket.write is one)
 * Convert socket errors to MongoNetworkErrors

h4. Testing Requirements
 * Assert a MongoNetworkError is thrown in the identified cases

h4. Documentation Requirements
 * None

h4. Follow Up Requirements
 * None

summary: Options required in rewrapManyDataKey() TS signature
description:
h3. Use Case

As a nodejs driver user
I want to be able to execute methods as they described
So that I can rely on documentation & typescript types

 
{code:java}
```
* @example
* ```ts
* // rewrapping all data data keys (using a filter that matches all documents)
* const filter = {};
*
* const result = await clientEncryption.rewrapManyDataKey(filter);
* if (result.bulkWriteResult != null) {
* // keys were re-wrapped, results will be available in the bulkWrite object.
* }
*
```
{code}
but options marked as required in the interface:
{code:java}
```
async rewrapManyDataKey(
filter: Filter<DataKey>,
options: ClientEncryptionRewrapManyDataKeyProviderOptions
): Promise<\{ bulkWriteResult?: BulkWriteResult }> {
```
{code}
h3. User Experience
 * Autogenerated documentation and typescript types match

h3. Dependencies
 * None

h3. Risks/Unknowns
 * None

h3. Acceptance Criteria
h4. Implementation Requirements
 * Mark options as optional

h4. Testing Requirements
 * TS test

h4. Documentation Requirements
 * N/A

h4. Follow Up Requirements
 * N/A

summary: OIDC re-authentication fails with promoteValues: false
description:
# 
 ## Expected behaviour

391 - Reauthenticate is handled within the driver by reauthenticating the user.

## 

391 - Reauthenticate error is thrown sometimes. We see this error in Compass, as reported by [https://jira.mongodb.org/browse/HELP-83883].

We were also able to reproduce this error with a 8.2-enterprise mongo server and a simple oidc flow.

Digging a bit deeper, the problem is caused by the error code sometimes being an {{Int32}} instead of a primitive value. 

This goes against {{MongoError}} type definition, which uses primitive types ({{{}string | number | undefined{}}} for {{code}} ).
And it lead to this check failing to catch the {{391}} as intended:
[node-mongodb-native/src/sdam/server.ts|https://github.com/mongodb/node-mongodb-native/blob/2512137b5d94b238e7cf5ee753f867e8d1e2e0fc/src/sdam/server.ts#L359]

Line 359 in [2512137|https://github.com/mongodb/node-mongodb-native/commit/2512137b5d94b238e7cf5ee753f867e8d1e2e0fc]
|operationError.code === MONGODB_ERROR_CODES.Reauthenticate|

.

As a... bundling user of the Node driver
I want... node-specific APIs (process.nextTick) to be replaced with non-node specific ones (queueMicrotask)
So that... bundling is easier

h3. Risks/Unknowns

h3. Acceptance Criteria
h4. Implementation Requirements
 * Review Node.js event loop fundamentals (specifically relative to nextTick and queueMicrotask)
 * Replace process.nextTick() with queueMicrotask() (or another alternative)
 * Ask Anna for review

h4. Testing Requirements
 * Existing tests pass

h4. Documentation Requirements
 * Release note

h4. Follow Up Requirements
 * Regression testing is deferred to a consolidated follow up ticket for M0

summary: Investigate errors in updated spec tests for "Check for timeout in tailable-awaitData unified spec tests"
description:
h3. Use Case
As a... Node developer
I want... to pick up the latest CSOT test specs and run them without errors
So that... we are consistent with the latest test specs.

h3. User Experience
 * _What is the desired/expected outcome for the user once this ticket is implemented?_

The latest CSOT test specs indicate that we should be short-circuiting socket timeouts by validating if maxAwaitTimeMS < operational timeout for tailable awaitData cursors.
At the moment, that is not the case for the Node driver: we are not checking that maxAwaitTimeMS < operational timeout. We allow the operation to succeed (if it happens quickly enough) or to face an imminent timeout error.

Instead, we shouldn't even be attempting the operation and should be quickly failing with a client-side non-timeout error, like we sometimes do in https://github.com/mongodb/node-mongodb-native/blob/main/src/cursor/abstract_cursor.ts#L296-L298:

{code:js}
throw new MongoInvalidArgumentError(
  'Cannot specify maxAwaitTimeMS >= timeoutMS for a tailable awaitData cursor'
);
{code}

Verification:
1. Add maxAwaitTimeMS/timeout implementation
2. Remove `NODE-7297` suppressions from `test/integration/client-side-operations-timeout/client_side_operations_timeout.spec.test.ts`
3. Run all spec tests
4. Everything should pass

h3. Risks/Unknowns
 * _What could go wrong while implementing this change? (e.g., performance, inadvertent behavioral changes in adjacent functionality, existing tech debt, etc)_
** If the check is done incorrectly, customers may see exceptions from new places


h3. Acceptance Criteria
h4. Implementation Requirements
* Throw MongoInvalidArgumentError when maxAwaitTimeMS/timeout are in conflict.

h4. Testing Requirements
* All tests are passing.
* No NODE-7297 skips

h4. Documentation Requirements
* N/A

h4. Follow Up Requirements
* N/A

summary: MongoDB 7.0.0 crashes on startup with BSON 7.1.0 due to missing NumberUtils.getInt32LE API
description:
h3. Use Case

As a developer using MongoDB driver v7.0.0  
I want the driver to work with its declared BSON dependency
So that my application starts without crashing

h3. User Experience
 * Users who install MongoDB 7.0.0 after the release of BSON 7.1.0 will be completely blocked.
If you are using Bun + TypeScript, you won't even be able to start the app.

h3. Dependencies
 * BSON 7.1.0

h3. Risks/Unknowns

- BSON 7.1.0 has breaking API changes despite minor version bump
- May behave differently in Bun vs Node.js
- Need to verify which BSON version is actually compatible with MongoDB 7.0.0

h3. Acceptance Criteria
h4. Implementation Requirements

1. Application starts successfully
2. MongoDB driver initializes without errors
3. Basic CRUD operations work

h4. Testing Requirements

- Verify application starts in Bun environment
- Test basic MongoDB CRUD operations
- Confirm no regression in Node.js environment

h4. Documentation Requirements

- N/A

h4. Follow Up Requirements

- Check if newer MongoDB driver version supports BSON 7.1.0
- File issue with MongoDB driver team about BSON compatibility
- Verify BSON semver compatibility policy with MongoDB team

summary: Aggregation with write stage not using provided read preference
description:
h3. Use Case

I have several aggregations using $out or $merge ("write stages") where I want to read from a secondary using readPreference: 'secondary'.

The provided readPreference is not used if the operation has a write stage in [execeute_operation|https://github.com/mongodb/node-mongodb-native/blob/287c98a9ee2e127ef5ddf124d730686f8296b0d5/src/operations/execute_operation.ts#L201] because topology.commonWireVersion is 0, resulting in the wrong custom server selector being returned in [server_selection|https://github.com/mongodb/node-mongodb-native/blob/287c98a9ee2e127ef5ddf124d730686f8296b0d5/src/sdam/server_selection.ts#L71] where it falls back to using ReadPreference.primary.

 

Root cause is probably commonWireVersion being initialised with 0 instead of null in [topology_description_ctor|https://github.com/mongodb/node-mongodb-native/blob/287c98a9ee2e127ef5ddf124d730686f8296b0d5/src/sdam/topology_description.ts#L69] and then not being updated in [topology_description_update|https://github.com/mongodb/node-mongodb-native/blob/287c98a9ee2e127ef5ddf124d730686f8296b0d5/src/sdam/topology_description.ts#L198]

 
h3. User Experience
 * _Desired outcome: The provided read preference is used when operation has write stage._
 * _Impact: All customers using aggregations with write stages and read preference 'secondary'. Falls back to primary read preference._

h3. Dependencies
 * _n/a_

h3. Risks/Unknowns
 * _n/a_

h3. Acceptance Criteria
h4. Implementation Requirements
 * _functional reqs, potential snafus to avoid, performance targets, etc_

h4. Testing Requirements
 * _unit test, spec test sync, etc_

h4. Documentation Requirements
 * _DOCSP ticket, API docs, etc_

h4. Follow Up Requirements
 * _additional tickets to file, required releases, etc_
 * _if node behavior differs/will differ from other drivers, confirm with dbx devs what standard to aim for and what plan, if any, exists to reconcile the diverging behavior moving forward_

summary: Enable more build warnings on Windows
description: This task is a follow-up of NODE-6265 and tracks adding the following flags to the product in order to satisfy internal Microsoft [BinSkim|https://github.com/microsoft/binskim] requirements: [/sdl|https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/cpp/build/reference/sdl-enable-additional-security-checks?view=msvc-170], [/W3|https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/cpp/build/reference/compiler-option-warning-level?view=msvc-170], and [/w34146|https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/cpp/error-messages/compiler-warnings/compiler-warning-level-2-c4146?view=msvc-170]. By adding these flags, node-gyp rebuild will build with more enabled warnings and emit error messages as needed. At this time, adding the flags does not result in any new build warnings.

summary: Refactor Crypto to Web Crypto API
description:
h3. Use Case

As a Node Driver Maintainer
I want the driver to use the standard Web Crypto API ({{{}globalThis.crypto{}}}).
So that the driver can be used in JS environments where the Node.js crypto module is not available.
h3. User Experience
 * Authentication works seamlessly in Edge runtimes that don't have node crypto module.

h3. User Experience
 * No changes in behaviour
 * Existing FIPS detection logic remains identical to previous versions.

h3. Risks/Unknowns
 * The Web Crypto API ({{{}subtle.digest{}}}, {{{}subtle.sign{}}}, {{{}subtle.deriveBits{}}}) is asynchronous (Promise-based). The current Node.js crypto usage is synchronous.
 * Retaining {{require('crypto')}} for {{.getFips()}} (even conditionally) can cause bundlers to include polyfills automatically.

h3. Acceptance Criteria
h4. Implementation Requirements
 * Replace {{{}randomBytes{}}}: Use {{crypto.getRandomValues()}} (Synchronous replacement).
 * Replace Hashing: Replace {{createHash}} and {{createHmac}} with {{crypto.subtle.digest}} and {{{}crypto.subtle.sign{}}}.
 * Replace PBKDF2: Replace {{crypto.pbkdf2Sync}} with {{crypto.subtle.deriveBits}} (Must be async).
 * Remove {{require('crypto')}} for all hashing/random/pbkdf2 operations.
 * Preserve the [crypto.getFips()|https://github.com/mongodb/node-mongodb-native/blob/main/src/cmap/auth/scram.ts#L236] where it will be conditionally imported on demand.

{code:javascript}
try {
  // Dynamic require to avoid crashing in non-Node envs
  if (require('crypto').getFips()) {
     throw new Error('Auth mechanism SCRAM-SHA-1 is not supported in FIPS mode');
  }
} catch (e) { /* ignore if module missing */ }
{code}
 ** As discussed in Tech Chat, we should keep SCRAM-SHA1 as Node-only
 * Comparison: implement a manual, constant-time timingSafeEqual function, as Web Crypto does not provide one.
 ** `timingSafeEqual` is not required by SCRAM-SHA1 spec, so we don't need to port/match it

h4. Testing Requirements
 * Verify existing SCRAM authentication tests pass.
 * Verify replacement functions generate the same output as node crypto module.
 * Verify that all modified crypto lines are covered in tests, and that these tests ran and passed

h4. Documentation Requirements
 * Add a section "Running in Custom Runtimes" to the Driver Readme explaining that users using Webpack/Vite may need to prevent {{crypto}} polyfill injection and note that {{getFips}} support is specific to the Node.js runtime.

h4. Follow Up Requirements
 * _additional tickets to file, required releases, etc_
 * _if node behavior differs/will differ from other drivers, confirm with dbx devs what standard to aim for and what plan, if any, exists to reconcile the diverging behavior moving forward_

summary: Unify node:dns `resolveSrv`, `resolveCname` and other methods with `resolve()`.
description:
h3. Use Case

As a... Node.js Driver Developer
I want... to refactor the driver's DNS layer to rely on the generic {{dns.promises.resolve(hostname, rrtype)}} method.
So that... I can delete the disparate logic handling specific methods ({{{}resolveSrv{}}}, {{{}resolveTxt{}}}, {{{}resolve4{}}}, {{{}resolve6{}}}) and support future "Pluggable I/O" providers with a significantly smaller API surface.
h3. User Experience
 * Code Reduction: The driver's internal {{DNSProvider}} interface simplifies from having 5+ methods to essentially 2:

 # 
 ## {{{}resolve(name, type){}}}: Handles SRV, TXT, and explicit A/AAAA queries via the network.
 ## {{{}lookup(name){}}}: Handles the initial connection address resolution (respecting OS hosts file).

h3. Dependencies
 * This is a direct prerequisite for {*}Milestone 1 (Pluggable I/O){*}. The defined {{NetworkInterface}} in the design document relies on this unified DNS shape.

h3. Risks/Unknowns
 * Node.js {{resolve(name, 'A')}} is *not* equivalent to {{{}lookup(name){}}}. The former ignores the OS hosts file; the latter respects it. Read more [here|https://nodejs.org/docs/latest/api/dns.html#implementation-considerations]
 ** _Risk:_ If we blindly replace {{lookup()}} with {{{}resolve(..., 'A'){}}}, local development setups (e.g., mapping {{db.local}} in {{{}/etc/hosts{}}}) will break.
 ** _Mitigation:_ The unified interface must still distinguish between "System Resolution" (lookup) and "Network Resolution" (resolve), OR we must implement a fallback mechanism.

h3. Acceptance Criteria
h4. Implementation Requirements
 - Identify all usages of {{{}resolveSrv{}}}, {{{}resolveTxt{}}}, {{{}resolve4{}}}, and {{{}resolve6{}}}.
 - Replace them with the generic {{{}dns.promises.resolve(hostname, rrtype){}}}.
 - Ensure that errors thrown by the generic {{resolve}} method are caught and handled consistently (Node.js generic {{resolve}} might return different error codes than specific helper methods in some edge cases).

_Example:_ Change {{resolveSrv(name)}} to {{{}resolve(name, 'SRV'){}}}.
h4. Testing Requirements
 * All tests pass.

h4. Documentation Requirements
 * Release note

h4. Follow Up Requirements
 * N/A

summary: Expose `cursor.bufferedCount` from `ChangeStream`
description:
h3. Use Case: Enable efficient batch-processing of change streams

We have been using change streams for some time now: we created a wrapper around a {{ChangeStream}} instance that deals with resume token management (incl. recycle/deployment scenarios). This works like a charm for some time now.

Recently, we introduced a new use case where we need more throughput. We have a working solution that adds configurable batching, but we needed to patch the {{ChangeStream}} class of the mongodb library to make it work efficiently. This ticket is a request to add that to the official package so we can stop using our {{{}yarn patch{}}}-ed version of the library.

Long story short: we need access to the (public) {{bufferedCount()}} function of the (private) {{ChangeStream.cursor}} field.

The {{bufferedCount}} value allows use to avoid triggering {{getMore}} (as {{tryNext}} does) calls during processing during "slower" moments, when the stream contains less changes.

{*}Note{*}: Using a {{ChangeStreamCursor}} directly would give us access to the {{{}bufferedCount(){}}}, but we prefer using the existing ChangeStream-based solution instead of reimplementing existing functionality.
h3. User Experience
 * _What is the desired/expected outcome for the user once this ticket is implemented?_
*_We have access to the  {{ChangeStream.bufferedCount}}_*

h3. Dependencies
 * _none_

h3. Risks/Unknowns

Since we are exposing the public {{AbstractCursor.bufferedCount()}} value, this seems like low/no-risk. Especially since {{ChangeStreamCursor.resumeToken}} is already exposed from {{ChangeStream.resumeToken}} is a similar way (we might as well expose the cursor directly, but that won't be needed).

We think it could be interesting to include this in all language drivers.
h3. Acceptance Criteria
h4. Implementation Requirements

Add {{ChangeStream.bufferedCount}}.
h4. Testing Requirements

Not sure if this would be relevant.
h4. Documentation Requirements

TypeScript docs can take care of this.

summary: Add driver metadata to mastra integration
description:
We released the ability to add metadata after mongoclient is initialized in [https://jira.mongodb.org/browse/NODE-6923]. 

See example of `appendMetadata` API here: [https://github.com/mongodb/node-mongodb-native/releases/tag/v6.18.0] 


We should add this telemetry tracking for both:
 * Vector search 
 * MongoDB Storage

Please ensure the telemetry format is aligned with driver team's guidance on AI frameworks telemetry tracking. 

Related ticket: [https://jira.mongodb.org/browse/NODE-7108]

summary: TLS connections lose TCP keepAlive after NODE-6245
Address ENGSEC-670: OIDC Host Allowlist Bypass via Improper Suffix Matching
EJSON.stringify() type signature doesn't match implementation logic
Use of scalar refs in combination with numbers seems to create trouble
Perl driver di
description:
I've written a test case which is attached. When I run this program, it fails around 60% of the time with:

  error getting database response: Resource temporarily unavailable

I have googled for "perl mongodb resource temporarily unavailable" with no relevant hits and I've been seeing this bug for quite a while. I've coded around this in my application(s), but is there any way to handle this condition other than Try::Tiny? Is there something I'm doing wrong? 

Thanks in advance for any assistance you can provide.

summary: valid count 0 and error not distinguishable in count result
description:
The current implementation of Cursor::count does not allow to distinguish a 0 result from an error.

In our case, the query coincided with a chunk move. The return document contained "ok" : 0, and a string for "errmsg". run_command returns the errormsg string. This in turn causes count to return 0.

summary: Error Condition documentation / handling -
description: Currently both $err or $errMsg are set separately. It may make sense to add an $errCode with the actual error code to all errors, which would enforce them being cataloged, and then have both $err and $errMsg populated to allow backward compatibility. Assuming users are checking both today, I would be OK just having one of them defined if that is considered a viable option.

summary: Change minHeartbeatFrequencyMS from 10 to 500
GridFS ignores "10014 chunk is empty!" assertion, returns empty string on corrupt files
Invalid use_boolean check
Implement CRUD write API
description: https://github.com/mongodb/specifications/tree/master/source/crud

summary: Add inserted IDs to BulkWriteResult
description: The original Bulk spec didn't require inserted IDs.  The new CRUD spec lists them as optional, but it would be good to add.

summary: Inserting documents as array ref allows duplicate keys
description: Will allow insert/update documents to be provided as an array reference and the code appears to send duplicate key/value pairs to the server, which I think has undefined behavior for duplicates (in practice it appears to take the last value).  We could convert arrays to hashes (losing order) or to IxHash (expensive but preserving order), or we could throw an error if the user tries to use that.

summary: deprecate 'last_error' method on Database class
description: GLE will be pretty much pointless with the shift to write commands.  The Database class should deprecate this method.

summary: Remove 'safe' option in CRUD operations
description: The CRUD spec doesn't allow for a per-operation write concern.  Having new methods not allow it but having old methods with a "safe" option is going to be confusing.  We should get rid of "safe" and rely on the collection-level write concern.

summary: Implement CRUD read API
description: https://github.com/mongodb/specifications/tree/master/source/crud

summary: Consistent ordered document argument validation/coercion
description:
[Github pull 36|https://github.com/mongodb/mongo-perl-driver/pull/36] points out that some functions, like Cursor::sort accept hashes or IxHash but not array references.

Wherever an ordered document is a valid argument, we should have consistent validation/coercion using a type constraint.

summary: Support all query modifiers
description: Not all query modifiers are supported by Cursor class methods.  See http://docs.mongodb.org/manual/reference/operator/query-modifier/
